# WP-G Resume — Structural Completion & Conceptual Massing

You are resuming a crashed agent session on the **bim-ai** repo
(`/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai`). bim-ai is a browser-based BIM authoring
tool (React + TypeScript + Three.js, Vite, Vitest). This prompt is
self-contained.

---

## Repo orientation (WP-G relevant paths)

```
packages/web/src/tools/toolRegistry.ts               — ToolId union + TOOL_REGISTRY
packages/web/src/viewport/meshBuilders.ts             — 3D mesh dispatcher
packages/web/src/viewport/meshBuilders.brace.ts       — brace 3D mesh (done)
packages/web/src/viewport/meshBuilders.brace.test.ts  — brace tests (done)
packages/web/src/viewport/meshBuilders.beamSystem.ts  — beam system 3D mesh (exists!)
packages/web/src/viewport/meshBuilders.beamSystem.test.ts — beam system tests (exists!)
packages/web/src/viewport/meshBuilders.massBox.ts     — box mass mesh (done)
packages/web/src/viewport/meshBuilders.massExtrusion.ts — extrusion mass mesh (done)
packages/web/src/viewport/meshBuilders.massRevolution.ts — revolution mass mesh (done)
packages/web/src/viewport/meshBuilders.massVolumes.test.ts — mass volume tests (done)
packages/web/src/viewport/meshBuilders.ramp.ts        — ramp mesh (belongs to WP-C)
packages/web/src/plan/bracePlanSymbol.ts              — brace plan symbol (done)
packages/web/src/plan/beamSystemPlanSymbol.ts         — beam system plan symbol (exists!)
packages/web/src/plan/massVolumePlanSymbol.ts         — mass volume plan symbol (done)
packages/web/src/plan/curtainWallPlanSymbol.ts        — curtain wall plan symbol (done)
packages/web/src/plan/columnAtGrids.ts                — column-at-grid helper (untracked!)
packages/web/src/plan/columnAtGrids.test.ts           — column-at-grid tests (untracked!)
packages/web/src/tools/massByFace.ts                  — roof/wall by face utilities (done)
packages/web/src/tools/massByFace.test.ts             — by-face tests (done)
packages/web/src/tools/massFloorsByLevel.ts           — floor-by-level utility (done)
packages/core/src/index.ts                            — Element union + ElemKind
```

Architecture patterns:
- All element types follow: define `kind` in core → implement 3D mesh builder
  in `viewport/meshBuilders.<type>.ts` → dispatch from `meshBuilders.ts` → add
  plan symbol in `plan/<type>PlanSymbol.ts` → call from `planProjection.ts`.
- Semantic commands: study `{ type: 'createWall', ... }` for shape conventions.
- Tests: co-located `*.test.ts`, run with `pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web`.
- Prettier runs automatically.

---

## What was done before the crash

| Sub-task | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| G1 Brace | **Done** | `'brace'` ToolId in registry; `meshBuilders.brace.ts`; `bracePlanSymbol.ts`; tests. Tracker: "Implemented — G1". |
| G2 Beam System | **Partial** | `meshBuilders.beamSystem.ts` + `beamSystemPlanSymbol.ts` + tests exist. `'beam-system'` ToolId NOT in `toolRegistry.ts`. `beam_system` kind IS in `core/index.ts`. Needs wiring. |
| G3 Column at Grids | **Partial** | `plan/columnAtGrids.ts` + `plan/columnAtGrids.test.ts` exist as **untracked files** (not committed). Options-bar "At Grids" button not wired. |
| G4 Sloped Columns | **Not Started** | Tracker: "Not Started — P2" |
| G5 Mass Volumes | **Done** | `mass_box`, `mass_extrusion`, `mass_revolution` in core + mesh builders + plan symbol. ToolIds `mass-box`, `mass-extrusion`, `mass-revolution` in toolRegistry (staged, not committed). Tracker: "Implemented". |
| G6 Roof by Face | **Done** | `massByFace.ts` utilities + `MassFaceRef` type. Tracker: "Implemented (G6)". |
| G7 Wall by Face | **Done** | `massByFace.ts` side-face utilities. Tracker: "Implemented (G7)". |
| G8 Floor by Level | **Done** | `massFloorsByLevel.ts` + tests. Tracker: "Implemented (G8)". |
| G9 Curtain Wall | **Partial** | `curtainWallData` compound type in core; `curtainWallPlanSymbol.ts`. No interactive grid editing UI. |

---

## Step 0 — commit staged + untracked work FIRST

There are **staged changes** not yet committed:
- `packages/core/src/index.ts` — `mass_box`, `mass_extrusion`, `mass_revolution` in ElemKind
- `packages/web/src/tools/toolRegistry.ts` — `'brace'` tool entry
- `spec/revit-parity/revit2026-parity-tracker.md` — tracker updates

There are **untracked new files** to stage as well:
- `packages/web/src/plan/columnAtGrids.ts`
- `packages/web/src/plan/columnAtGrids.test.ts`

Verify, test, then commit:
```bash
git pull --rebase origin main
pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web -- columnAtGrids
git add packages/core/src/index.ts \
        packages/web/src/tools/toolRegistry.ts \
        spec/revit-parity/revit2026-parity-tracker.md \
        packages/web/src/plan/columnAtGrids.ts \
        packages/web/src/plan/columnAtGrids.test.ts
git commit -m "feat(structural): brace tool + mass ElemKind types + columnAtGrids helper (WP-G)"
git push origin main
```

---

## What still needs to be done

### G2 — Wire the Beam System tool

The 3D mesh and plan symbol exist. The `beam_system` element kind is in core.
Still needed:

1. **Add `'beam-system'` ToolId** to `toolRegistry.ts` (hotkey `BS`, modes:
   plan + 3D) — rebase first since `toolRegistry.ts` is shared.

2. **Grammar** in `tools/beam-system.ts` (or `toolGrammar.ts`):
   - Draw a closed boundary polygon (click points, double-click/Enter to close)
   - Options bar: spacing (default 1200mm), direction (angle, default: longest
     boundary edge), profile picker
   - On complete: dispatch `{ type: 'createBeamSystem', boundaryPoints, spacingMm, beamDirection, profileId, levelId }`

3. **Inspector** for `beam_system` elements: spacing, direction, profile,
   boundary vertex grips.

4. **Verify existing tests** in `meshBuilders.beamSystem.test.ts` pass after
   wiring. Add grammar tests if missing.

5. **Update tracker** §9.3 from "Not Started" to at least "Partial".

### G3 — Wire "At Grids" button for column placement

`columnAtGrids.ts` logic exists (just committed). Still needed:

1. When the `'column'` tool is active, add an **"At Grids" button** to the
   options bar (`tools/OptionsBar.tsx`).

2. **Grammar extension**: clicking "At Grids" switches the column tool into
   a grid-selection mode:
   - User clicks/box-selects grid lines
   - Enter triggers: compute all pairwise intersections (reuse `columnAtGrids.ts`)
   - Dispatch N `createColumn` commands for each intersection point

3. Options bar: column type, base level, top level (same for all batch placements).

4. Tests: 3 horizontal × 3 vertical grids → verify 9 columns at correct XY positions.

5. **Update tracker** §9.1.2 from "Not Started" to at least "Partial".

### G4 — Sloped / Inclined Columns

Not started. Add support for non-vertical columns:

1. **Data model** — add to column element in `core/index.ts`:
   - `topOffsetXMm?: number` — horizontal X offset of column top from base
   - `topOffsetYMm?: number` — horizontal Y offset of column top from base

2. **3D mesh** — update the column case in `meshBuilders.ts`: when
   `topOffsetXMm` or `topOffsetYMm` is non-zero, extrude along the inclined
   axis instead of straight up.

3. **Plan symbol** — show base footprint (solid) + top footprint (dashed, offset)
   connected by a diagonal line.

4. **Inspector** — top offset X/Y fields; read-only computed inclination angle.

Tests: column with `topOffsetXMm: 500`, verify 3D mesh top vertices are offset
correctly from base.

### G9 — Curtain Wall interactive grid editing

`curtainWallData` type and plan symbol exist. Add the authoring UI:

1. **Wall type picker**: add a "Curtain Wall" category with preset types
   ("Storefront", "Curtain Wall", "Exterior Glazing") distinguished by their
   `curtainWallData` defaults.

2. **Inspector** when a curtain wall is selected: H grid (count/spacing),
   V grid (count/spacing), panel type dropdown, mullion type dropdown.

3. **"Edit Grid" mode**: in plan view, clicking a curtain wall enters grid-edit
   mode. The user can click to add/remove grid lines at exact positions along
   the wall. Click outside to exit.

4. **3D rendering**: route curtain walls to the curtain panel builder in
   `meshBuilders.ts` instead of the solid wall builder. The test file
   `meshBuilders.curtainPanels.test.ts` should guide this.

5. **Plan rendering**: curtain walls show as thin line with short perpendicular
   tick marks for each grid division (update `curtainWallPlanSymbol.ts` or
   `planProjection.ts`).

Tests:
- `meshBuilders.curtainWall.test.ts`: 4m × 3m curtain wall, 3 vertical + 2
  horizontal divisions → 6 glass panels rendered
- `curtainWallPlanSymbol.test.ts`: verify plan projection shows grid tick marks

---

## Rules

- `git pull --rebase origin main` before every major sub-task
- Commit + push after each completed sub-task
- Do NOT touch annotation/dimension, stair/ramp (WP-C), export (WP-E),
  phase dialogs (WP-F), groups (WP-B), view/sheet rendering (WP-D)
- `toolRegistry.ts` is shared — always rebase before editing
- `pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web` before each push
- Update `spec/revit-parity/revit2026-parity-tracker.md` as you complete items

# WP-G Resume Prompt — Structural Completion & Conceptual Massing

You are resuming a crashed agent session. Read the full original spec at
`spec/revit-parity/agent-prompts/WP-G-structural-and-massing.prompt.md` for
architecture patterns and the definition of done. This prompt tells you what
was already done and what still needs implementing.

## State of the repo when you pick up

Run `git pull --rebase origin main` first.

**There are staged and untracked changes you must handle first:**

### 1. Staged changes (not yet committed)

`packages/core/src/index.ts` has `mass_box`, `mass_extrusion`, `mass_revolution`
added to the `ElemKind` union (G5 scaffolding).

`packages/web/src/tools/toolRegistry.ts` has the `'brace'` ToolId + registry
entry added (G1 scaffolding — hotkey BR, plan+3D modes).

`spec/revit-parity/revit2026-parity-tracker.md` has tracker updates for G1
(marked "Implemented") and several WP-A annotation tools (marked Partial).

Commit these staged changes:
```bash
git add packages/core/src/index.ts packages/web/src/tools/toolRegistry.ts \
        spec/revit-parity/revit2026-parity-tracker.md
git commit -m "feat(structural): add brace ToolId + mass ElemKind types (WP-G scaffold)"
git push origin main
```

### 2. Untracked new files (not staged)

```
packages/web/src/plan/columnAtGrids.ts
packages/web/src/plan/columnAtGrids.test.ts
```

These are the G3 (Column at Grid Intersections) implementation. Read them,
run the tests, fix any issues, then stage and commit:
```bash
pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web -- columnAtGrids
git add packages/web/src/plan/columnAtGrids.ts \
        packages/web/src/plan/columnAtGrids.test.ts
git commit -m "feat(structural): column-at-grid-intersections helper + tests (G3 partial)"
git push origin main
```

## What was completed before the crash

- **G1 Partial** — `'brace'` ToolId + registry entry added to toolRegistry
  (staged), tracker marks it "Implemented". Verify the mesh builder
  (`viewport/meshBuilders.brace.ts`) and plan symbol (`plan/bracePlanSymbol.ts`)
  actually exist. If they don't, implement them (full spec in original prompt).
- **G3 Partial** — `columnAtGrids.ts` + test file exist untracked. Read them
  to assess completeness; the options-bar "At Grids" button wiring may be
  missing.
- **G5 Scaffolded** — `mass_box`, `mass_extrusion`, `mass_revolution` ElemKind
  types added to core (staged). No mesh builders or grammars yet.
- **G9 Done** — Curtain wall `curtainWallData` compound type added to wall
  element + plan symbol committed (`feat(G9)`).

## What still needs implementing (in priority order)

### G1 — complete brace implementation

ToolId is in the registry (once committed). Verify or implement:

  line (reuse beam sweep logic)
- `plan/bracePlanSymbol.ts` — dashed diagonal line + X symbol mid-span

### G3 — complete column-at-grids wiring

- Grammar: click "At Grids" → multi-select grid lines → Enter → dispatch N
  `createColumn` commands for each pairwise intersection
- Options bar: column type, base level, top level

### G2 — Beam System (Auto-Fill Structural Bay)

New ToolId `'beam-system'` (hotkey BS). Data model with `beam_system` element
storing boundaryPoints, beamDirection, spacingMm, profileId. Grammar: draw
closed boundary polygon → options bar (spacing, direction, profile).

- Tests: co-located *.test.ts, run with pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web.

WHAT WAS DONE BEFORE THE CRASH:
All ToolIds and grammar state machines are registered and committed:

- A2: leader-text (LT) — grammar only. No renderer, no persistence.

input.command:
python3 - <<'EOF'
import re

with open('/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/revit-parity/revit2026-parity-tracker.md') as f:
    content = f.read()

# Find all status lines - look for the pattern used in tracker entries
done = len(re.findall(r'\*\*Status:\s*Done', content, re.IGNORECASE))
partial = len(re.findall(r'\*\*Status:\s*Partial', content, re.IGNORECASE))
not_started = len(re.findall(r'\*\*Status:\s*Not Started', content, re.IGNORECASE))
implemented = len(re.findall(r'\*\*Status:\s*Implemented', content, re.IGNORECASE))
na = len(re.findall(r'\*\*Status:\s*N/A', content, re.IGNORECASE))

total_trackable = done + partial + not_started + implemented
print(f"Done:        {done}")
print(f"Implemented: {implemented}")
print(f"Partial:     {partial}")
print(f"Not Started: {not_started}")
print(f"N/A:         {na}")
print(f"")
print(f"Trackable total (excl N/A): {total_trackable}")
print(f"Done+Implemented: {done+implemented} ({(done+implemented)/total_trackable*100:.1f}%)")
print(f"Done+Impl+Partial: {done+implemented+partial} ({(done+implemented+partial)/total_trackable*100:.1f}%)")
print(f"Not Started: {not_started} ({not_started/total_trackable*100:.1f}%)")

# Break down by chapter
print("\n--- By Chapter ---")
chapters = re.split(r'## Chapter (\d+)', content)
for i in range(1, len(chapters), 2):
    ch_num = chapters[i]
    ch_body = chapters[i+1]
    d = len(re.findall(r'\*\*Status:\s*Done', ch_body, re.IGNORECASE))
    imp = len(re.findall(r'\*\*Status:\s*Implemented', ch_body, re.IGNORECASE))
    p = len(re.findall(r'\*\*Status:\s*Partial', ch_body, re.IGNORECASE))
    ns = len(re.findall(r'\*\*Status:\s*Not Started', ch_body, re.IGNORECASE))
    t = d + imp + p + ns
    if t > 0:
        pct = (d+imp)/t*100
        print(f"Ch {ch_num:>2}: Done/Impl={d+imp:>2} Partial={p:>2} NotStarted={ns:>2} | {pct:.0f}% done ({t} items)")
EOF

content.text:
**27% fully done, 86% touched in some way, 14% not started at all.**

Of 195 trackable items (excluding N/A):

- **Done / Implemented: 53 (27%)**
- **Partial: 115 (59%)** — exists but parity incomplete
- **Not Started: 27 (14%)**

Weakest chapters:

| Chapter | Done | Gap |
|---|---|---|
| Ch 1 — UI & Startup | 5% | Mostly Partial, 19 items still shallow |
| Ch 4 — Annotations | 5% | A1–A12 grammar landed but renderers/persistence still Partial |
| Ch 5 — Terrain/Geo | 10% | Terrain editing and true north mostly Partial |
| Ch 9 — Structural | 9% | Beam systems, sloped columns, steel not started |
| Ch 12 — Export | 18% | DXF/IFC complete, but 4 items (DWG, IFC import, PDF/point cloud) untouched |

Strongest:

- **Ch 11 — Massing: 80%** (G5–G8 just landed)
- **Ch 2 — Basic floor plan: 57%**
- **Ch 8 — Advanced walls/stairs: 35%**

The honest summary: the WP-A through WP-G sprint moved the needle from roughly 15% to 27% fully done, and eliminated most of the "Not Started" items in the targeted chapters. The big remaining work is turning the 115 **Partial** items into **Done** — that's the bulk of the project.

content.text: This is a thorough audit — I'll spawn a dedicated agent to cross-reference every tracker item against the actual codebase.

input.description: Deep audit of revit-parity tracker vs actual codebase
input.prompt:
You are doing a deep audit of the bim-ai codebase at /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai.

The goal: cross-reference every item in the Revit 2026 parity tracker against what actually exists in the codebase. We want to catch three problems:
1. Items marked "Not Started" that already have real implementation (tracker is stale)
2. Items marked "Partial" that are actually more complete than stated, OR where a parallel/duplicate implementation exists
3. Items marked "Done/Implemented" that are not actually wired into the running app (dead code, not called from anywhere)

The tracker is at: spec/revit-parity/revit2026-parity-tracker.md

Work through all 15 chapters systematically. For each tracker item, do targeted codebase searches (grep, ls, read relevant files) to determine whether the implementation actually exists and is wired. Focus especially on:
- "Not Started" items that might secretly exist
- "Partial" items where you suspect more is done
- Any obvious duplication (two files doing the same thing)

Key codebase paths to know:
- packages/web/src/plan/ — 2D plan canvas, plan symbols, tools
- packages/web/src/viewport/ — Three.js 3D, mesh builders
- packages/web/src/tools/ — tool registry, grammar, OptionsBar
- packages/web/src/export/ — exporters
- packages/web/src/workspace/ — workspace shell, sheets, project browser
- packages/web/src/groups/ — model groups
- packages/web/src/clipboard/ — clipboard/paste
- packages/web/src/phases/ — phase management
- packages/web/src/schedules/ — schedule views
- packages/web/src/levels/ — level management
- packages/web/src/inspector/ — inspector panels
- packages/web/src/state/ — Zustand store
- packages/core/src/index.ts — all Element types and ElemKind union

Be specific in your findings. For each discrepancy say:
- Which tracker section (e.g. §9.3)
- What the tracker currently says
- What you actually found in the codebase (file names, line references)
- Verdict: tracker is STALE (should be upgraded), DUPLICATE (two implementations), UNWIRED (exists but not connected), or ACCURATE

Produce a structured report grouped by chapter. Flag the highest-priority corrections clearly. This is research only — do not edit any files.
